_meta.description: Notification service rules: routing, idempotency, and channel abstraction for GEM India.
sources: AGENTS.md — 'Every notification send checks the idempotency key in Redis before sending'
research-hub/BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md — Notification log schema, resilience infrastructure

routing_rule.statement: All notifications MUST go through lib/notifications/. Never call Resend or Evolution API directly from a page or API route.

structure.whatsapp.ts: Evolution API adapter (swap to WABA later)
structure.templates/: Template registry mapping template IDs to channels

idempotency_rule.statement: Every notification send MUST check the idempotency key in Redis before sending. Duplicate sends MUST NOT dispatch a second notification.
idempotency_rule.source: AGENTS.md — 'Every notification send checks the idempotency key in Redis before sending'; research-hub/BACKEND_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md — Resilience infrastructure

cascade_triggers.notification: notify affected faculty with revised responsibilities

open.issue: Provider calls inline, no timeout/circuit breaker

negative_tests.description: Send same notification twice with same idempotency key — second call MUST NOT dispatch
Call Resend/Evolution API directly from a page component — code review MUST flag as violation
Trigger cascade event and verify notification is routed through lib/notifications/

open_questions.question: What is the exact response behavior when a duplicate notification send is attempted? Success-with-noop or explicit duplicate-rejection?
